page.title: Encryption & Hashing
page.lead: Encryption and hashing are security pillars in backend systems. They protect credentials, reduce impact during leaks, and help meet baseline security requirements for modern APIs.

concepts.title: What are they and why do they matter?
concepts.description: <mark class="docs-highlight">Encryption</mark> transforms data into a reversible format (with a key) to protect sensitive information in transit or at rest. <mark class="docs-highlight">Hashing</mark> transforms data into a non-reversible digest and is especially used for passwords.
concepts.warn: For passwords, use brute-force resistant hashing (scrypt, bcrypt, or argon2). Do not store passwords with reversible encryption.

hasherExample.title: Hasher example (Skyguard)
hasherExample.description: Skyguard includes <mark class="docs-highlight">Hasher</mark> with scrypt, random salt, and support for verification and progressive rehash.

hasherBatch.title: Batch processing
hasherBatch.description: For migrations or bulk validations, use <mark class="docs-highlight">Hasher.hashBatch()</mark> and <mark class="docs-highlight">Hasher.verifyBatch()</mark> with controlled concurrency.

bcryptUsage.title: Common bcrypt functions
bcryptUsage.description: This example covers <mark class="docs-highlight">genSalt</mark>, <mark class="docs-highlight">hash</mark>, <mark class="docs-highlight">compare</mark>, and their sync variants.
bcryptUsage.tip: In production, prioritize async bcrypt functions to avoid blocking the event loop.
